_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize federal-House batch: Kelly Morrison (MN-03 incumbent), Thomas J. Smith (MI-08 R challenger, no presence), Tripp Adams (MI-10 D, WITHDREW), Gena Ross (MO-06 R, party-switcher), Josh Smead (MO-06 D). Every TRUE/FALSE cites a source; null where no evidence; never inferred from party.

profile.confidence_note: Sitting MN-03 Rep (sworn Jan 3 2025), running for reelection in the Aug 11 2026 D primary; scored from recorded House votes, bill cosponsorships, and single-issue-group endorsements. Heritage Action lifetime score 0%.

kelly-morrison.notes_set: Kelly Morrison — incumbent U.S. Rep, MN-03 (D), OB-GYN. Documented progressive record: EMILY's List 'pro-choice champion', cosponsor of the Women's Health Protection Act (H.R.12, codifies abortion) and the Equality Act (H.R.15, LGBTQ/gender-identity). Voted NO on the SAVE Act (H.R.22, proof-of-citizenship voter registration) and NO on the Laken Riley Act (H.R.29, detention of illegal aliens charged with theft). Giffords-endorsed; backs an assault-weapons ban, universal background checks, red-flag orders, and repeal of the gun-manufacturer liability shield. Heritage Action 0% lifetime. No cross-party deviation found. Religious-liberty, economic, foreign-policy, and health-freedom cells null — no specific federal evidence located.

0.note: Pro-choice OB-GYN; EMILY's List 'pro-choice champion'; cosponsor of WHPA (H.R.12) codifying abortion access — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Cosponsors abortion-codifying legislation rather than abolition.

4.note: Endorsed/backed by EMILY's List, an abortion-advocacy PAC.

0.note: Cosponsor of the Equality Act — does not affirm marriage as man-woman only.

1.note: Equality Act cosponsor; supports LGBTQ nondiscrimination, not opposition to same-sex marriage.

2.note: Equality Act extends protections on gender identity — does not reject transgender ideology.

4.note: Equality Act promotes LGBTQ identity in public policy.

1.note: Voted NO on the SAVE Act (H.R.22), which would require documentary proof of citizenship for voter registration.
Voted NO on the Laken Riley Act (H.R.29) mandating detention of illegal aliens charged with theft.

0.note: Giffords-endorsed; backs gun-control regime, not constitutional carry.

1.note: Supports an assault-weapons ban, universal background checks, and red-flag/extreme-risk protection orders.

2.note: Supports repealing the gun-manufacturer liability shield; favors more federal gun regulation, not repeal of NFA/GCA.

4.note: Giffords-aligned gun-control agenda is contrary to opposing citizen-disarmament initiatives.

profile.confidence_note: Roster-verified, still on the Aug 4 2026 MI-08 GOP primary ballot, but exhaustive search (FEC, Ballotpedia, WDET/Politics1 voter guides, web/social) found NO campaign website, social media, FEC financial filing, questionnaire, or endorsements. No citable policy position on any issue — all cells null; nothing fabricated.

thomas-j-smith-mi-08.notes_set: Thomas J. Smith — declared Republican candidate in the Aug 4 2026 MI-08 primary challenging Rep. Kristen McDonald Rivet (D). Confirmed still on the ballot (WDET 2026 voter guide; Politics1) with no withdrawal found. Essentially zero public campaign presence: no website, no social media, no FEC financial report on file, no candidate questionnaire, no endorsements located. No scoreable issue positions exist — all cells null pending any future public statements.

profile.confidence_note: WITHDREW / suspended campaign late July 2025 (cited mental health) and endorsed rival Christina Hines; no longer on the certified Aug 4 2026 MI-10 Democratic primary ballot. Currency fix is the result; ran ~6 weeks with no substantive policy record (slogans only).

tripp-adams.notes_set: Tripp Adams — Army/Navy veteran, U-M law grad, of Utica MI; entered the open MI-10 race (John James's former seat) June 16 2025 and SUSPENDED his campaign late July 2025 citing his mental health, endorsing Democratic rival Christina Hines ('the leader that Macomb and Oakland counties need'). As of mid-2026 the certified MI-10 Democratic primary field (Chung, Greimel, Hines, Jaye) no longer lists Adams. Status set not_running. Campaign ran ~6 weeks; website now down; never completed a Ballotpedia Candidate Connection survey; only slogan-level economy/health/jobs/education content existed — no scoreable issue positions. All cells null.

profile.confidence_note: Verified active 2026 Republican primary candidate for the open MO-06 seat (FEC H6MO06286, SoC filed Aug 12 2025; primary Aug 4 2026 vs. Jim Ingram). PARTY-SWITCHER: 2020 Democratic nominee MO-06 and 2022 Democratic US Senate candidate. Her only detailed policy record is Democratic-era (pre-2024) and treated as NON-CURRENT; the 2026 GOP campaign offers slogans only — all current cells null, nothing fabricated.

gena-ross.notes_set: Gena Ross — verified active 2026 Republican primary candidate for the open MO-06 seat (Sam Graves's; FEC H6MO06286; primary Aug 4 2026 vs. Jim Ingram). MAJOR PARTY-SWITCHER: she was the 2020 DEMOCRATIC nominee for MO-06 and a 2022 DEMOCRATIC US Senate candidate (former staffer for Sen. Claire McCaskill). Her only substantive documented platform is Democratic-era (Medicare for All, fully-funded public schools without school choice, gun background checks/waiting periods) and is treated as non-current per evidence discipline — NOT scored as 2026 GOP positions. Her 2026 campaign shows only slogans ('Common Sense, Not Party Line'; 'conservative because I believe in personal responsibility and freedom'). No 2026 issue-group endorsements or questionnaire located. All current cells null pending fresh 2026 statements.

profile.confidence_note: Verified active 2026 Democratic primary candidate for the open MO-06 seat (FEC H6MO06260; Aug 4 2026 primary vs. Matthew Levine). Scored from his own campaign Issues page (votesmead.com/issues) and local press. Notable cross-party deviation flagged: affirmative pro-Second-Amendment language plus term-limits support.

josh-smead.notes_set: Josh Smead — verified active 2026 Democratic candidate for the open MO-06 seat (architect, Liberty MO; FEC H6MO06260; primary Aug 4 2026 vs. Matthew Levine). Policy-rich record from his own Issues page: pro-choice ('A Woman's Right to Choose'), pro-LGBTQ ('Everyone deserves to live freely and equally', supports the ERA), anti-school-choice ('Public education funding belongs in public schools, not private or religious institutions'), due-process immigration ('resources focused on courts instead of mass detention centers'). Documented credit where earned: opposes ag monopolies squeezing family farmers (industry_capture). CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS flagged: affirmative pro-2A framing ('the Second Amendment... works to keep tyrants at bay'), supports term limits, and a draft that includes 'all able bodied Men & Women' — but these are general statements that don't map cleanly to specific gun-policy cells, so those are null.

0.note: 'A Woman's Right to Choose... Missourians deserve sovereignty over their own bodies' — pro-choice, does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Pro-choice stance is contrary to abortion abolition.

4.note: 'LGBTQ+ Rights... Everyone deserves to live freely and equally — no exceptions'; supports the ERA — affirmatively promotes LGBTQ identity in public policy.

0.note: 'Public education funding belongs in public schools, not private or religious institutions' — opposes school choice.

1.note: 'Immigration enforcement should be... centered on due process, with resources focused on courts instead of mass detention centers' — opposes mandatory deportation/detention.

2.note: 'Missouri farmers... are being squeezed by monopolies' — opposes Big Ag consolidation and supports family-farm protection against agricultural cartels.
